name: Flag Brazil
desc: The Brazilian flag with green, yellow, and blue featuring stars.

name: Soccer Ball
desc: Brazilian football (futebol), the country's most beloved sport.

name: Beach with Umbrella
desc: Copacabana and Ipanema, the famous beaches of Rio de Janeiro.

name: Performing Arts
desc: Carnival in Rio de Janeiro, the world's biggest party.

name: Woman Dancing
desc: Samba, Brazil's energetic dance from Rio's Carnival.

name: Cut of Meat
desc: Churrasco, Brazilian barbecue with grilled meats.

name: Meat on Bone
desc: Picanha, Brazil's favorite cut of beef for barbecue.

name: Shallow Pan of Food
desc: Feijoada, Brazil's national dish of black beans and pork.

name: Dumpling
desc: Coxinha, a popular Brazilian chicken croquette snack.

name: Cheese Wedge
desc: Pão de queijo, Brazilian cheese bread, a popular snack.

name: Hot Beverage
desc: Brazilian coffee, the largest coffee producer in the world.

name: Cup with Straw
desc: Caipirinha, Brazil's national cocktail made with cachaça.

name: Palm Tree
desc: Palm trees along Brazil's extensive coastline.

name: Deciduous Tree
desc: The Amazon Rainforest, which covers much of Brazil.

name: Monkey
desc: Monkeys in the Amazon and Atlantic forests of Brazil.

name: Parrot
desc: Colorful parrots found in Brazil's rainforests.

name: Leopard
desc: The jaguar, the largest big cat in the Americas, found in Brazil.

name: Cherry Blossom
desc: Ipê, Brazil's national flower tree with vibrant yellow blooms.

name: Snow-Capped Mountain
desc: Sugarloaf Mountain, the iconic peak overlooking Rio de Janeiro.

name: Moai
desc: Christ the Redeemer statue overlooking Rio de Janeiro.

name: Stadium
desc: Maracanã Stadium in Rio, one of the world's largest football stadiums.

name: Musical Notes
desc: Bossa nova, Brazilian music genre made famous by Tom Jobim.

name: Guitar
desc: The Brazilian guitar used in samba and bossa nova music.

name: Small Airplane
desc: Air travel to Brazil, a top tourist destination.

name: Desert Island
desc: Fernando de Noronha, Brazil's stunning island archipelago.

name: Water Wave
desc: The Atlantic Ocean along Brazil's coastline.

name: Sun
desc: Brazil's tropical and subtropical climate with year-round warmth.
